challenges.title: Exception Handling
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that uses exception handling. Write a function `divide(int a, int b)` that throws a runtime_error when b is zero, and returns a / b otherwise. In main(), call the function inside a try block and catch the exception, printing an error message. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Include a divide function that throws when dividing by zero
- Use try, catch, and throw keywords
- Print an error message when the exception is caught
challenges.test_code:
assert 'throw' in cpp, 'Should use throw to raise an exception'
assert 'try' in cpp, 'Should have a try block'
assert 'catch' in cpp, 'Should have a catch block to handle the exception'
assert 'divide' in cpp, 'Should define a divide function'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Should have a main function'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'main should return 0'
hints: Use: if (b == 0) throw runtime_error("Division by zero");
Wrap the call in: try { divide(10, 0); } catch (runtime_error& e) { cout << e.what(); }

challenges.title: Function Template and STL Vector
challenges.description:
Write a C++ program that defines a function template `findMax` that accepts two parameters of the same type and returns the larger value. Also create a vector of integers, add at least 3 values using push_back, and use a loop to print them. Assign your C++ code to the variable `cpp`.

Your program must:
- Define a function template using the template keyword
- Use a vector from the STL with push_back
- Call findMax with at least one pair of values
challenges.test_code:
assert 'template' in cpp, 'Should use the template keyword to define a function template'
assert 'findMax' in cpp, 'Should define a function called findMax'
assert 'vector' in cpp, 'Should use an STL vector'
assert 'push_back' in cpp, 'Should add elements to the vector using push_back'
assert cpp.count('push_back') >= 3, 'Should push_back at least 3 values into the vector'
assert 'main()' in cpp or 'main ()' in cpp, 'Should have a main function'
assert 'return 0' in cpp, 'main should return 0'
hints: Define the template: template <typename T> T findMax(T a, T b) { return (a > b) ? a : b; }
Create a vector: vector<int> nums; then use nums.push_back(10);
